name: Credit.Org - Jacksonville, Fl Branch
description_short: National nonprofit credit counseling and HUD-approved housing counselor network offering free debt relief, credit building, and homeownership guidance since 1974.
description_long:
Credit.org is an established nonprofit organization that has provided financial counseling and housing guidance for nearly 50 years. Founded in 1974, the organization has completed over 5.2 million counseling sessions and delivered $2.2 billion in homeowner support across its national network, including the Jacksonville, FL branch. The organization operates as a HUD-certified housing counselor network with NFCC certification, positioning it within the legitimate nonprofit credit counseling sector.

Credit.org offers a comprehensive suite of free and low-cost services spanning debt relief, housing assistance, credit building, and financial education. Services include one-on-one counseling for debt management and settlement options, reverse mortgage counseling (both HECM and non-HECM), pre-purchase homeownership education, foreclosure prevention assistance, rental readiness programs, and credit repair guidance. The organization provides free initial consultations followed by personalized financial plans. Beyond counseling, they offer the FIT Academy (self-paced online courses), live webinars and classes, financial calculators, and workplace financial wellness events. Multiple contact channels are available including phone (800-431-8157 or 800-431-8695), online appointment scheduling, and chat support.

What distinguishes Credit.org is its dual focus on both credit/debt counseling and HUD-certified housing counseling under one organization. The reverse mortgage counseling capability (a federally required service before obtaining a reverse mortgage) is a specialized offering. Their scale—5.2 million completed sessions—suggests operational maturity. The organization provides bilingual services (English/Spanish) and maintains transparent, educational messaging without aggressive sales tactics typical of for-profit debt companies. Client testimonials highlight counselor expertise and thoroughness.

The honest assessment is that Credit.org's Jacksonville branch is a legitimate, nonprofit credit counseling service appropriate for consumers seeking unbiased guidance. However, the website provides limited Jacksonville-specific information—it appears to be a national organization's standard website. The extent of in-person services at the specific Jacksonville location versus remote counseling is unclear. Like most nonprofits, their impact depends on counselor quality, which varies by individual appointment rather than being standardized across the network.
pros: HUD-certified and NFCC-affiliated nonprofit status ensures no-pressure, unbiased financial guidance
Free initial consultation and financial assessment with personalized plans—no upfront fees charged
Specialized reverse mortgage counseling (HECM and non-HECM), a federally required service before obtaining reverse mortgages
Comprehensive service range covering debt, housing, credit, foreclosure, rental assistance, and disaster recovery in single organization
50-year operational history with 5.2+ million completed counseling sessions demonstrates scale and experience
Free educational resources including FIT Academy courses, live webinars, and financial calculators available to all
Bilingual services (English/Spanish) and multiple contact options (phone, chat, online scheduling)
cons: Website is generic national template with no Jacksonville branch-specific information, staff directory, or local contact details
Unclear whether services are delivered in-person at Jacksonville location or primarily via remote counseling
No pricing transparency for any services beyond 'free initial consultation'—unclear what paid services might cost
Limited detail on counselor qualifications or credentials beyond generic NFCC affiliation; individual counselor quality appears variable
No mention of typical counseling timelines, follow-up schedules, or what 'personalized plan' specifically includes
best_for: Homebuyers seeking free pre-purchase education and credit counseling before applying for mortgages
Reverse mortgage inquirers needing federally required counseling before proceeding with application
Individuals with multiple debts seeking nonprofit guidance to evaluate consolidation, management, or settlement options without sales pressure
diagnosis: Credit.org is best for consumers seeking unbiased, nonprofit credit counseling and housing education without sales pressure or hidden fees. The primary caveat is that their Jacksonville location appears to be part of a national network using generic marketing materials, so actual service availability, counselor quality, and delivery format (in-person vs. remote) at the specific Jacksonville branch should be confirmed directly before scheduling.
services: Free one-on-one debt relief and debt management counseling with personalized financial plans
Credit building and credit repair guidance with credit score counseling
Credit report review and dispute assistance for errors and negative items
Pre-purchase homeownership counseling and down payment education
Reverse mortgage counseling (HECM and non-HECM required counseling)
Rental readiness and rental assistance counseling
FIT Academy self-paced online courses on debt, credit, budgeting, homeownership, and foreclosure prevention
Live interactive webinars and classes on credit, debt, and housing topics
Financial calculators for budgeting, debt management, and homeownership planning
Workplace financial wellness events and seminars for employers
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
